name: Sarawak (Malaysia)

colors.symbolism: the supremacy of law, order, and unity
the courage and sacrifice of Sarawak's peoples

description: A field divided diagonally — yellow above a black-and-red bend — with a nine-pointed star in the upper hoist canton.
history: Sarawak, on the northern coast of Borneo, became part of Malaysia in 1963. The current flag was adopted in 1988. The colours and the nine-pointed star reference Sarawak's nine administrative divisions and its multi-ethnic population.

features.symbolism: Sarawak's flag: yellow upper field with a diagonal black-and-red band; a nine-pointed yellow star in the upper hoist representing the nine administrative divisions of Sarawak.
